element.name: Roentgenium
element.summary: Roentgenium is a synthetic chemical element; it has symbol Rg and atomic number 111. It is extremely radioactive and can only be created in a laboratory. The most stable known isotope, roentgenium-282, has a half-life of 130 seconds, although the unconfirmed roentgenium-286 may have a longer half-life of about 10.7 minutes. Roentgenium was first created in December 1994 by the GSI Helmholtz Centre for Heavy Ion Research near Darmstadt, Germany. It is named after the physicist Wilhelm Röntgen, who discovered X-rays. Only a few roentgenium atoms have ever been synthesized, and they have no practical application.
element.etymologyDescription: Wilhelm Conrad Röntgen, discoverer of X-rays

group.label: Coinage metals
group.description: Copper, silver, gold, and roentgenium form group 11 -- the coinage metals. Their malleability, lustre, and resistance to oxidation have made them symbols of value for millennia.
